top_signals.title: Tanker Market Stays Elevated in May
top_signals.summary: Dirty tanker spot freight rates remained elevated in May, although down from the record highs registered in March, OPEC said in its latest monthly report. Increased long-haul demand supported VLCC spot freight rates. The repositioning of tankers made more available in the Atlantic Basin, easing upward pressure on rates. top_signals.title: Retrofitting at scale: Lessons from delivering the world’s first methanol mega containership conversion
top_signals.summary: The retrofitting of a 20,000 TEU container vessel to run on methanol was successfully completed in China in 2025. With valuable lessons already learned, this can pave the way for further conversion of the global fleet to low-GHG fuels, supporting wider decarbonization efforts. The world’s first methanol conversion project for a mega container carrier was ...

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text

top_signals.title: Trump ban on AI access to foreign users forces Anthropic to suspend models
top_signals.summary: President Trump has banned non-American nationals from accessing Anthropic’s newest AI model, forcing the company to suspend the model for all users. Anthropic, which is the maker of Claude, said the US government cited national security for its banning order before it pulled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 from use. The decision has raised concern [...]

top_signals.title: GDP (Second Estimate) and Corporate Profits, 1st Quarter 2026
top_signals.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 1.6 percent in the first quarter of 2026 (January, February, and March), according to the second estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the fourth quarter of 2025, real GDP increased 0.5 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the first quarter were exports, investment, consumer spending, and government spending. Imports, which are a subtraction in the calculation of GDP, increased. Full Text

top_signals.title: Dangote ramp-up slashes WAF product imports, reshapes clean tanker routes: analysts
top_signals.summary: The ramp-up of Nigeria’s 650,000 b/d refinery has cut imports of clean products to West Africa and is redirecting tanker flows around the region, according to analysts. West African imports of clean refined products fell to 765,000 b/d in May from 997,000 b/d in April, a 23% decline, S&P Global Commodities at Sea(opens in a ...
